content_markdown: The $P$-$V$ diagram shows two isotherms for a fixed mass of ideal gas, at temperatures $T_X$ and $T_Y$. Which **one** of the following is correct?

diagram_metadata.description: A pressure-volume (P-V) graph showing two hyperbolic curves representing isotherms. The upper curve is labeled T_Y and the lower curve is labeled T_X.

options.label: D
options.text: Temperatures cannot be compared from a $P$-$V$ diagram alone.

vibe_explanation: According to the ideal gas law $PV = nRT$, for a fixed amount of gas, the product $PV$ is directly proportional to the absolute temperature $T$. On a $P$-$V$ diagram, isotherms further from the origin represent higher values of $PV$ and thus higher temperatures. Since the curve for $T_Y$ is consistently above and to the right of the curve for $T_X$, it follows that $T_Y > T_X$.
